ogTitle: Milsons Passage vs Milton: Climate Safety

heading: Milsons Passage vs Milton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Milsons Passage, New South Wales and Milton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Milton has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Milsons Passage (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Milsons Passage or Milton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Milsons Passage has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Milton scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Milsons Passage scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Milton scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Milsons Passage or Milton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Milton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
